owners.name: Stewart Scriver
owners.notes: Special education teacher turned antique dealer, still repairs jewelry

owners.name: Patricia Scriver
owners.notes: Had tenure at University of Toronto

owners.name: Cece Scriver
owners.notes: Was 2 years old when store opened, now runs operations

owners.name: Owen
owners.notes: Former chef, now works at the store

services: Vintage clothing (50s-80s cocktail dresses, tweed blazers, cashmere)
Handmade & antique beads (glass, carved bone, gemstone, from around the world)
Silver jewelry from Mexico and Asia
Handmade buttons from Indonesian family company
Antique & repurposed jewelry (Stewart crafts one-of-a-kind pieces)
Luchador masks, scarves, incense, leather goods, patches
notable_facts: Celebrating 50 years in 2025 — Kensington Market institution since 1975
Celebrity clientele includes Blondie and royalty
Entire family lives in Kensington Market, walks to work
Stewart still repairs & customizes vintage jewelry at 86
First day sales exceeded $100, never dropped below that daily
Website is a redirect with no real content (couragemylove.com redirects to /lander, returns 403)
customer_perception: Organized and clean unlike other vintage shops
Separated into little stalls for jewelry, beads, incense, scarves
Back section with its own attendant for beads and small jewelry
International treasures from global sourcing trips
